methodology_sections: 2.3 Data Flow Tracing - Input flow, external data, asset movement, permission escalation
3.2 Business Logic Analysis - Economic incentives, edge cases, assumption violations, race conditions

path: GenesisPoolManager.depositToken() receives genesisPool address and amount
GenesisPool.depositToken() validates pool status and timing
Transfers funding tokens from spender to GenesisPool
transformations: _getNativeTokenAmount converts deposit to native via auction oracle
_getFundingTokenAmount converts native to funding via auction oracle
sinks: Funding token balance in GenesisPool contract
data_flows.trust_changes: User-provided genesisPool address is not validated against factory - any address could be passed
exposure_risks: GenesisPoolManager.depositToken() does not verify genesisPool is from factory - user could pass a malicious contract
manipulation_risks: Attacker could pass a malicious genesisPool address that returns true from depositToken, triggering _preLaunchPool on arbitrary pool
No validation that genesisPool was created by genesisFactory

path: Sender provides nativeToken, auctionIndex, genesisPoolInfo, allocationInfo
Checks pair exists and has zero balances
Gets or creates genesis pool from factory
Transfers native tokens from sender to genesisPool
Selects auction from factory by index (falls back to index 0)
sinks: GenesisPool stores genesisInfo, allocationInfo, auction address
data_flows.trust_changes: User-provided auctionIndex is used to select auction; if out of range returns address(0) which falls back to auctions[0]
exposure_risks: If auctions array has a removed (zeroed) entry at index 0, fallback to auctions(0) returns address(0)
manipulation_risks: Token owner can re-deposit to same genesis pool after NOT_QUALIFIED status since getGenesisPool returns address(0) for NOT_QUALIFIED, triggering createGenesisPool again with fresh state
If auctionFactory.auctions(auctionIndex) returns address(0) AND auctions(0) also returns address(0), auction is set to address(0) - calls to auction will revert or behave unexpectedly

path: _launchPool sets genesis status false, sets gauge genesis pool
Gauge.depositsForGenesis() stakes LP with maturity lock
transformations: Native and funding tokens converted to LP tokens via addLiquidity
data_flows.trust_changes: Router is trusted for fair exchange rate; zero slippage allows sandwich attacks
exposure_risks: Zero slippage protection in addLiquidity allows front-running/sandwich attacks
Router address set by governance but setRouter has inverted check (requires _router == address(0))
manipulation_risks: MEV attacker can sandwich the addLiquidity call to extract value since amountAMin and amountBMin are both 0
If pair already has liquidity from another source, addLiquidity ratio could be manipulated

path: Token owner provides incentive tokens and amounts
Validates each token is native or connector
Transfers tokens from sender to GenesisPool
Records in incentives mapping and incentiveTokens array
Later, transferIncentives() approves and sends to external bribe
transformations: Transferred to external bribe via notifyRewardAmount
sinks: External bribe contract receives incentive tokens
data_flows.trust_changes: Token trust assumed standard ERC20; fee-on-transfer tokens would cause accounting mismatch
validation_points: Token must be native or connector
exposure_risks: Fee-on-transfer tokens: amount recorded != amount received
Duplicate incentive tokens allowed - push to array each time incentives[_token] == 0, but if claimed and re-added, array grows
manipulation_risks: If fee-on-transfer token is used as incentive, recorded amount exceeds actual balance, causing later transfers to fail or steal from other pools

data_flows.source: Token owner or depositors claiming refunds
path: claimNative(): tokenOwner claims refundable native tokens
claimDeposits(): depositors claim funding tokens when NOT_QUALIFIED
claimIncentives(): tokenOwner claims incentive tokens when NOT_QUALIFIED
data_flows.trust_changes: No reentrancy guard on GenesisPool claim functions
exposure_risks: claimIncentives does not zero out amounts before transfer in the loop - sets to 0 one by one in order, potential reentrancy via callback tokens
manipulation_risks: If incentive tokens have transfer callbacks (ERC777), reentrancy in claimIncentives could re-enter and claim again since incentives are zeroed per-iteration

path: propose() creates proposal with hashed params
castVote() allows voting during active period
_execute loops through targets calling each with value
transformations: Proposal ID computed from targets, values, calldatas, epochTimeHash
data_flows.trust_changes: Any proposal in Succeeded, Defeated, or Expired state can be executed
validation_points: State must be Succeeded, Defeated, or Expired
exposure_risks: Defeated and Expired proposals can be executed - critical governance flaw
manipulation_risks: Attacker can execute defeated proposals to run arbitrary governance actions
Expired proposals (no quorum reached, vote not succeeded/defeated) are also executable

path: Gauge calls deductAmount with account and gaugeTokenAmount
transformations: gaugeTokenAmount converted to userAmount via proportion
exposure_risks: Division by zero if liquidity is 0 or 1 (since _depositerLiquidity = liquidity / 2)
Potential underflow in userDeposits[account] -= userAmount if accounting is off
manipulation_risks: If liquidity is odd, liquidity/2 truncates, causing _depositerLiquidity to be smaller than half, affecting accounting ratios
For tokenOwner: gaugeTokenAmount >= pendingOwnerStaked triggers subtraction that may underflow if rounding causes userAmount to be less than the pendingOwnerStaked portion

data_flows.trust_changes: Quorum uses current total supply instead of historical, allowing manipulation
validation_points: None - no timestamp passed to getsmNFTPastTotalSupply
exposure_risks: In Governor.sol, quorum() ignores blockTimestamp parameter and calls getsmNFTPastTotalSupply() without timestamp
This means quorum is based on current supply, not snapshot supply, making it manipulable
manipulation_risks: Attacker can increase total supply to raise quorum threshold, preventing proposals from passing
Or decrease supply to lower quorum, making it easier to pass malicious proposals

core_rules: Genesis pools follow 8-stage lifecycle: DEFAULT -> NATIVE_TOKEN_DEPOSITED -> PRE_LISTING -> PRE_LAUNCH -> PRE_LAUNCH_DEPOSIT_DISABLED -> LAUNCH/PARTIALLY_LAUNCHED/NOT_QUALIFIED
Deposits only allowed during PRE_LISTING or PRE_LAUNCH after startTime
Threshold determines minimum allocation for launch eligibility
Token owner gets refundable native tokens on partial launch or disqualification
Depositors get refunds only on NOT_QUALIFIED status
Liquidity is split 50/50 between depositors and token owner for balance tracking
Governance proposals require quorum and vote success to execute
economic_incentives: Token owners provide native tokens and incentives to attract depositors
Depositors exchange funding tokens for proportional native token allocation
Incentives are forwarded to external bribe to attract gauge votes
LP tokens are maturity-locked in gauge to prevent immediate dumping
governance_mechanisms: smNFT-based voting power for governance proposals
Quorum fraction determines minimum participation requirement
Voting period with delay, active period, and execution

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should require _router != address(0) to prevent setting router to zero
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Requires _router == address(0), meaning only address(0) can be set as router, effectively bricking router updates
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Owner cannot update router to a valid address. If current router becomes compromised or needs upgrading, the protocol is stuck. Setting router to address(0) would cause all launch operations to revert.

edge_cases.scenario: Governor execute() allows execution of Defeated and Expired proposals
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Only Succeeded proposals should be executable
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Execute requires status == Succeeded || status == Defeated || status == Expired, allowing defeated and expired proposals to be executed
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Any defeated governance proposal can be executed, completely undermining the governance model. An attacker can submit a malicious proposal, have it voted down, and still execute it.

edge_cases.scenario: GenesisPoolManager.depositToken() does not validate genesisPool address against factory
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should verify genesisPool was created by the genesis factory
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Any non-zero address is accepted as genesisPool, calling depositToken on it
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Attacker deploys malicious contract that implements IGenesisPool interface, returns true from depositToken() to trigger _preLaunchPool(), which creates a gauge for the attacker-chosen pool address from getLiquidityPoolInfo()

edge_cases.scenario: Zero slippage protection in addLiquidity during launch
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should have minimum amount parameters to prevent sandwich attacks
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: MEV bot can front-run the addLiquidity call, manipulate the pool price, and extract significant value from the genesis pool launch

edge_cases.expected_behavior: quorum should use historical total supply at proposal snapshot time
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Calls token.getsmNFTPastTotalSupply() without passing blockTimestamp, using current supply
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Quorum threshold can be manipulated by changing total supply between proposal creation and vote deadline

edge_cases.scenario: Governor.sol _voteSucceeded requires forVotes > abstainVotes (in Governor.sol variant)
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Standard governance: forVotes > againstVotes should be sufficient
edge_cases.actual_behavior: In Governor.sol L2GovernorCountingSimple, _voteSucceeded requires forVotes > againstVotes AND forVotes > abstainVotes
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Abstain votes can be weaponized to block proposals even when they have majority support over against votes

edge_cases.scenario: totalDeposits can be zero when balanceOf() or deductAmount() is called
edge_cases.actual_behavior: balanceOf divides by totalDeposits which could be zero, causing revert
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If totalDeposits is 0 but liquidity is set (theoretically shouldn't happen in normal flow), balanceOf() reverts with division by zero

edge_cases.scenario: liquidity / 2 truncation for odd liquidity values
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Exact accounting for all LP tokens
edge_cases.actual_behavior: _depositerLiquidity = liquidity / 2 loses 1 wei for odd values; the remaining 1 wei LP token is unaccounted
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Dust amount (1 wei) of LP tokens permanently locked in gauge, negligible economic impact

edge_cases.scenario: Fee-on-transfer tokens used as funding or incentive tokens
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Actual received amount should be tracked
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Amount parameter is stored directly without checking actual received balance
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If funding token has fees, totalDeposits will exceed actual balance, causing last withdrawer to fail or getting less than expected allocation

edge_cases.scenario: incentiveTokens array grows unboundedly with addIncentives
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Each new token is pushed to array; also if incentives are claimed and re-added for a new pool cycle, array keeps growing
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Gas griefing: token owner can add many tiny incentives to bloat the array, making claimIncentives and transferIncentives consume excessive gas

edge_cases.scenario: checkBeforeEpochFlip iterates and removes from liveNativeTokens while iterating in reverse
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Iterates in reverse (i from length to 1) and _removeLiveToken swaps last element. When removing index i-1, the last element replaces it, but since iterating backwards, the replaced element at i-1 won't be visited if i decrements
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If two consecutive pools at end of array both need action, the replacement from pop could cause one to be skipped in the same epoch

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Deposits should be blocked within the NO_GENESIS_DEPOSIT_WINDOW before epoch end
edge_cases.actual_behavior: depositToken only checks block.timestamp >= genesisInfo.startTime; the deposit window closing is handled at manager level via checkBeforeEpochFlip changing status to PRE_LAUNCH_DEPOSIT_DISABLED
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If checkBeforeEpochFlip is not called in time, deposits can continue up to the epoch flip, potentially front-running the launch

edge_cases.scenario: liveNativeTokensIndex not cleaned up on removal
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Index mapping should be deleted for removed token
edge_cases.actual_behavior: _removeLiveToken does not delete liveNativeTokensIndex[nativeToken] for the removed token, only updates the replacing token's index
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Stale index remains in mapping but functionally inert since the array no longer contains the token

edge_cases.scenario: GenesisPool constructor does not set poolStatus
edge_cases.actual_behavior: poolStatus is not explicitly set in constructor, defaults to 0 which is PoolStatus.DEFAULT
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: No issue - default value matches expected initial state

assumption_violations: setRouter requires _router == address(0), making it impossible to set a valid router - likely a typo (should be !=)
Governor execute() assumes only Succeeded proposals should execute but code allows Defeated and Expired
FixedAuction assumes msg.sender is always a valid GenesisPool that implements getGenesisInfo/getAllocationInfo
addLiquidity assumes no front-running with 0 slippage tolerance
depositToken in manager assumes genesisPool parameter is legitimate without factory verification
claimIncentives assumes incentive tokens are standard ERC20 without callbacks
GenesisPool assumes auction address remains valid throughout lifecycle
game_theory_risks: Token owner can set extremely high startPrice through auction, making native tokens appear more valuable to depositors
Last-second deposits before PRE_LAUNCH_DEPOSIT_DISABLED status can game the allocation
Token owner controls incentive distribution and could add then remove incentives (via pool rejection) to manipulate voter behavior
Governance voting can be gamed by splitting smNFTs to vote from multiple addresses (if delegation is per-address)
Defeated proposal execution means governance votes are meaningless - any proposal submitted will eventually execute
integration_risks: Router.addLiquidity may return different amounts than desired due to existing pool state
External bribe contract (IBribe) interaction in transferIncentives uses safeApprove which may fail if previous approval is non-zero
Gauge.depositsForGenesis is trusted to correctly implement maturity lock
TokenHandler.isConnector and whitelistToken are trusted external calls
Auction contract is user-selectable by index, potentially using unsuitable pricing mechanism
PairFactory.setGenesisStatus trusted to correctly restrict trading during genesis
calculation_errors: deductAmount: userAmount = (totalDeposits * gaugeTokenAmount) / _depositerLiquidity may not precisely reverse the balanceOf calculation due to integer rounding, causing small accounting discrepancies
balanceOf: _depositerLiquidity = liquidity / 2; balance = (_depositerLiquidity * userDeposits[account]) / totalDeposits - truncation means sum of all balanceOf may not equal total liquidity
_eligbleForPreLaunchPool threshold calculation: (proposedNativeAmount * threshold) / 10000 - threshold is described as '100 * of original to support 2 decimals', meaning 50% = 5000, which is correct
Governor.sol quorum() function in L2GovernorVotesQuorumFraction does not use blockTimestamp parameter at all, always uses current total supply

token_semantics.notes: User-provided token for genesis pool. Fee-on-transfer tokens not accounted for in deposit/allocation logic. SafeERC20 used.
Must be a connector token per tokenHandler.isConnector(). Fee-on-transfer would break totalDeposits accounting. SafeERC20 used.
Must be native token or connector. Fee-on-transfer causes accounting mismatch in incentives mapping. Callback tokens (ERC777) could enable reentrancy in claimIncentives.
Standard ERC20 with owner-only mint/burn. Initial supply 1M tokens.
Standard ERC20 with configurable name/symbol/supply and owner-only mint/burn.
ERC721 NFT with 3-round minting (whitelist, whitelist batch, public). Uses sequential tokenIds from totalSupply().

invariants.name: total_deposits_conservation
invariants.notes: totalDeposits should equal sum of all userDeposits. Violated if fee-on-transfer tokens used.

invariants.name: liquidity_accounting
invariants.notes: liquidity/2 assigned to depositors, liquidity - liquidity/2 to tokenOwner. 1 wei dust possible for odd liquidity.

invariants.name: refundable_native_conservation
invariants.notes: refundableNativeAmount = proposedNativeAmount - allocatedNativeAmount for partial launch, = proposedNativeAmount for rejection.

invariants.name: pool_status_monotonicity
invariants.notes: Pool status should only transition forward through lifecycle. setPoolStatus from manager can set arbitrary status.

invariants.name: governance_proposal_execution_integrity
invariants.notes: VIOLATED: Defeated and Expired proposals can be executed due to execute() state check bug.
